title: NOC 13101 – Property Administrators | GTR Immigration
description: Property Administrators play a key role in managing residential and commercial real estate portfolios across Canada. Classified under NOC 13101, this occupation continues to see steady nationwide demand and offers viable immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker programs and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training

common_job_titles: Accommodation officer Apartment rental agent Housing project manager Property administrator Property leasing coordinator Property rentals manager Landlord/landlady
duties: Negotiate or approve rentals and lease agreements
Prepare and manage service contracts for maintenance and security
Coordinate repairs, renovations, and building upkeep
Track operating income and expenses and prepare financial reports
Review rental pricing to match market conditions
Respond to tenant service requests and complaints
Hire and supervise leasing agents and building staff
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school (except in British Columbia)
Language proficiency testing in British Columbia
Minimum age requirement of 19 in British Columbia
Training in property, strata, or real estate management
Several years of administrative experience in property-related roles
Strata manager licensing in British Columbia
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 17,000 new positions for Property Administrators, compared with 16,300 job seekers—indicating balanced but positive prospects for overseas applicants who plan their province selection carefully.
additional_information: Property Administrators are employed by real estate firms, strata management companies, property developers, and government agencies across the country.

Continued hiring in residential and commercial real estateStrong rental-market growth in major citiesEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $32,800 to $84,100 CAD
